title: Pouteria francii
taxonomy: Pouteria francii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This classification places it within the broader category of sapotes, a diverse group of tropical plants. The genus Pouteria, to which Pouteria francii belongs, is native to tropical regions of the world, particularly in Central and South America. The Sapotaceae family encompasses a wide range of plant species, many of which are valued for their edible fruits. Pouteria francii's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within this family of plants.

intro: Pouteria francii, commonly known as the Balsam Apple or Tamanu, is a plant species in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in French Polynesia. The health benefits of Pouteria francii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and promote skin healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and fatty acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
